programmes.show_description: ...to the Orchestra: Let Bill be your guide as he joins forces with maestro Anne Dudley and the BBC Concert Orchestra for a humorous fusion of music and comedy at the Royal Albert Hall.
Cafe Tavolini: In Bridgeport, Connecticut, Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that's being neglected as the couple who run it teeter on the edge of divorce.
Alan Johnson and his sidekick Dave Owen host at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton on Merseyside. Things get off to a hairy start with some scandalous sheets.
Nick and Gina Mead welcome their rivals to the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley, where the afternoon entertainment is a freezing competition out on the locks.
Brock hopes that his sense of humour and perfectionist nature will impress his guests at York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire. But his reluctance to go off-menu at breakfast disappoints.
Hospitality veterans Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little host at Heyford House in Oxfordshire. One guest settles some scores during an afternoon of boxing.
The B&Bers meet for one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid. After accusations of over-enthusiastic hosting, the winner is announced.
Taking inspiration from Coventry Cathedral, Keith and Marj create a Victorian-style parlour bathroom, complete with a giant candlestick holder and Marj's dream lampshade.
In Essex, there are deep-fried jam sandwiches and some attention-grabbing burlesque, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
Swim Neighbors: Carrie refuses to have a shared garden with the neighbours, so they spite her by building a swimming pool, which proves too tempting for Doug...
S'No Job: Uncle Stu asks Doug to get his son Danny a job at IPS, but Doug doesn't want to work with his cousin. Arthur takes part in film audience research.
The Incredible Lightness of Being a Baby: Cletus becomes a helium tycoon. Maggie reunites with baby Hudson from Playdate with Destiny.
Bart the Bad Guy: When Bart sees the superhero movie of the summer before anyone else, he uses his 'spoiler powers' to blackmail the town.
Can Menna Fitzpatrick replicate her golden glory of 2018 in the first run of the Slalom? Plus: all the best action from the penultimate day at the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics.
Menna Fitzpatrick goes for gold in Run 2 of the Slalom. Plus: all the best action from the penultimate day at the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics.
As the Para Cross Country and Alpine Skiing Slalom action concludes, the excitement builds for the start of the Mixed Team Wheelchair Curling gold medal match.

programmes.show_name: F1 Chinese GP Sprint Race and...
programmes.show_description: ...Qualifying Highlights: An eventful Qualifying session in Melbourne saw Mercedes lock out the front row, with George Russell on pole. Can the Silver Arrows carry the momentum to Shanghai?
Dabi and Devi can't wait to swap the grey skies of Croydon for the sunshine and heat in the seaside town of Gandia in Valencia. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £180k budget can buy.
Phil catches up with Chloe, who wanted to fly the nest to be near buzzy Caversham, and Ella and Grant, who needed something spectacular to tempt Grant out of his mum's house.
A lifeboat is converted into a camping pod. A mum and three daughters create a charming straw bale house. And George enjoys an oriental delight on the Isle of Wight.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Ade Adepitan has all the best action, with a veritable dump of skiing medals on the slopes. Plus: enjoy the slide at the Wheelchair Curling Mixed Team gold medal match.

programmes.show_name: New: The Race for Ancient Egypt...
programmes.show_description: ...in Colour: In 1922 the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb sparks global mania, while Egypt fights for independence, provoking a fascinating struggle for control of the ancient past. (Ep1/2)

programmes.show_name: New: Roman Empire by Train with...
programmes.show_description: ...Alice Roberts: In Nimes, Alice keeps running into crocodiles and Roman landmarks better than any outside of Rome itself. Then she explores Narbonne and hops on a train to Spain. (Ep5)
Bill leaves the bustle of the city behind and seeks solace in the beauty of mountainous Da Lat. With his head in the clouds, Bill finds love in unexpected places. (Ep3)
(2021) Tom McCarthy's crime thriller starring Matt Damon as an all-American, still-waters-run-deep oil worker from Oklahoma who travels to Marseilles to try to get his daughter out of jail.
(2021) Romantic drama with Odessa Young and Olivia Colman. In 1920s England a writer working as a maid has a passionate affair with the rich boy next door. It's not considered a good match.
Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Her partner Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claire's schedule? (Ep3)
Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London-based youth worker to the core. (Ep4)
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting in Derby and Nottingham for single mum Jo and adrenaline junkies Liz and John, who want a home big enough for all their sporting equipment.
Last Exit to Springfield: Homer takes on Mr Burns in a labour struggle that rapidly spirals out of control after Homer leads the workers on strike.
So It Has Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show: Bart's special April Fools' Day prank seriously misfires when the whole house explodes.
Pregnant Pause (Part 1/2): Carrie's shocked to discover that she's pregnant. And, with characteristic bad timing, Arthur wants Doug to build him a bathroom in the basement.
Pregnant Pause (Part 2/2): Doug struggles to hold down two jobs to save money for the baby's arrival. And Arthur still wants a new bathroom.
Screenless: The Simpsons have a digital detox, but one of them gets serious withdrawal symptoms - and, surprisingly, it's not Bart or Homer...
Better Off Ned: When Ned successfully mentors Bart, Homer gets jealous. So he mentors Nelson.
Neil Simpson is in contention for a medal in the Slalom final. Then the Men's Cross-Country Skiing Sitting 20km marks the culmination of Scott Meenagh's distinguished Paralympics career.
Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens and Fred Warburton fight for medals as the Men's Slalom reaches its climax. Dom Allen is in a medal hunt of his own in the Standing Slalom. Plus: Para Ice Hockey.
Mercedes secured a commanding one-two last time out in Melbourne, with George Russell taking the chequered flag. But Ferrari put up a spirited scrap. Can they challenge again in Shanghai?
The competition begins at Craig and Greta's Horseshoe Hotel in North Yorkshire. The guests love their eco approach to hosting but think that some of the facilities are lacking.
The B&Bers head to The Dixie Arms in Leicestershire, run by super-welcoming host Alex. Two guests are impressed by the blingy beds, but emotions run high at feedback time.
Matt hosts the week's third visit at Bradbury's of Blackpool, where he hopes to change perceptions of the seaside town. Will his supersized breakfast swing the competition in his favour?
The B&Bers go glamping at Little Oakhurst in Stonyhurst, near Clitheroe, run by Andy and Joanne. The views are spectacular, but it soon becomes apparent that glamping isn't for everyone...
There's plenty of score settling to be done on payment day as the B&Bers dissect the week, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced.
Horseman David Jones tackles some bothersome bracken. Trebah Garden's head gardener creates a delightful stumpery. Nigel Cooper sails along the Dart for an annual competition.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Ceremony: Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey, Sean Rose and Alice Tai present live coverage of the spectacular closing ceremony and look back at the Games.
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
(2022) Hugh Jackman, Vanessa Kirby, Laura Dern, Zen McGrath and Anthony Hopkins star in Florian Zeller's domestic drama about a father struggling to deal with his teenage son's depression.
